description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust wooden handle and two large, symmetrical steel blades extending from either side. The blades are broad, with a curved cutting edge and a pointed tip at the top, giving the axe a formidable appearance. The handle is cylindrical, slightly tapered, and features subtle carved rings for grip enhancement. The steel blades are matte gray, contrasting with the rich brown of the wooden shaft. The axe head is mounted securely atop the handle, with no visible fasteners or decorative elements. The overall design is utilitarian, suggesting it is intended for heavy-duty use rather than display.
description: A double-headed battle axe with a wooden handle and broad, matte gray steel blades. The design is robust and functional, with a pointed tip and carved grip rings.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical blades and the pointed tip, with the full handle visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle, with the pointed tip prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying both blades and the handle's back side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the handle's side.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with carved grip rings, medium thickness, no visible curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical steel blades with broad curved edges and a central pointed tip, no beard or poll, functional design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted atop the handle, no visible fasteners.
